title: Protective layers for metal electrode batteries
abstract: Hybrid electrodes for batteries are disclosed having a protective electrochemically active layer on a metal layer. Other hybrid electrodes include a silicon salt on a metal electrode. The protective layer can be formed directly from the reaction between the metal electrode and a metal salt in a pre-treatment solution and/or from a reaction of the metal salt added in an electrolyte so that the protective layer can be formed in situ during battery formation cycles.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A rechargeable battery comprising a hybrid electrode, wherein the hybrid electrode includes an electrochemically active metal or metal salt protective layer directly on an electrochemically active metal electrode. 2. The rechargeable battery of claim 1 , wherein the protective layer comprises an electrochemically active metal selected among Sn, In, Si, Al, Mg, or Ge. 3. The rechargeable battery of claim 1, wherein the protective layer comprises an electrochemically active metal salt of Sn, In, Si, Al, Mg, or Ge. 4. The rechargeable battery of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the metal electrode comprises substantially metallic lithium, metallic sodium, metallic aluminum, metallic zinc, metallic silicon, or metallic tin. 5. The rechargeable battery of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the protective layer has a thickness in the range of about 5 nm to about 500 microns. 6 The rechargeable battery of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the hybrid electrode is an anode of the battery and the battery further comprises, a cathode and an electrolyte. 7. The rechargeable battery of any one of claims 1 or 3-6, wherein the electrolyte includes a metal salt that forms the protective layer and optionally wherein the metal salt is in an amount of between about 10 mM to about 100 mM in the electrolyte. 8. The rechargeable battery of claim 1-2 or 4-7, wherein the protective layer comprises an electrochemically active metal selected among Sn or In and the metal electrode is selected from lithium or sodium metal. 9. The rechargeable battery of claim 8, wherein the hybrid electrode comprises a protective layer of Sn on a sodium metal electrode and can cycle stably for over 1000 hours in a carbonate-based liquid electrolyte. 10. A method of preparing a hybrid electrode, the method comprising: forming a protective layer on a metal electrode wherein the protective layer is electrochemically active. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the protective layer is formed by ion-exchange of a metal salt onto the metal electrode. 12. The method of claim 11 , wherein the metal salt is a tin or indium metal salt. 13. The method of claim 11, wherein the protective layer is formed directly from a reaction between the metal electrode and the metal salt in a pre-treatment solution. 14. The method of claim 11, wherein the protective layer is formed directly from a reaction between the metal electrode and the metal salt in an electrolyte of a battery. 15. The method of claim 10, wherein the protective layer is formed by any one or more of the following: laminating an alloy layer directly on a metal anode; mechanically pressing, physically rolling or pressing from a dummy electrode, an alloy layer directly on a metal anode; depositing, sputtering or vapor depositing, an alloy layer directly on a metal anode; or coating, as spin coating an alloy layer directly on a metal anode. 16. A rechargeable battery comprising a hybrid electrode, wherein the hybrid electrode includes a silicon salt on a metal layer of the electrode. 17. The rechargeable battery of claim 16, wherein the silicon salt is silicon chloride and the metal layer comprises substantially metallic lithium or metallic sodium. 18. A method of preparing the hybrid electrode of any one of claims 16-17, comprising adding a silicon salt to an electrode of the battery. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the amount of silicon salt added to the electrolyte is less than 1.0 M. 20. The method of claim 18, wherein the amount of silicon salt added to the electrolyte is less than 0.5 M.
